title: salix caprea subsp. aurita
intro: Salix caprea subsp. aurita, also known as Kevling or Goat Willow, is a subspecies of the Goat Willow tree (Salix caprea), characterized by its distinctive, twigs with numerous small shoots, and is native to Europe and western Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Salix caprea subsp. aurita have been shown to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves can be made into a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, or used as a tincture or salve to apply topically to affected areas.
